svg.text: Selection steers the update — it does not push harder
Every training round moves the weights almost the same distance, whether the judge's selection pressure that round was strong or absent.
Weight step = size of this round's merged LoRA update minus last round's (gauge-invariant). Selection gap = mean risk of kept answers minus the pool's mean risk.
A. Per-round weight step against that round's selection pressure
selection gap magnitude this round (kept-minus-pool mean risk, absolute value)
weight step this round, as a multiple of its family’s average step
OLMo risk model, K2 grid + modal cells
each dot: one training round of one run
correlation of step size with gap magnitude:
value, as a multiple of its own average
Every training round, regardless of how selective the judge was, the merged LoRA update travels almost the same distance: OLMo steps
average 1.68 with a coefficient of variation of 0.13, Qwen steps average 0.86 with a coefficient of variation of 0.09. Step size is nearly
uncorrelated with that round's selection gap (r = 0.10 OLMo, r = 0.23 Qwen) and with the alignment-times-supply force term (r = 0.02
OLMo). Meanwhile the behavioral output of the same runs — the total pool-risk move over the whole run — spans 0.00 to 0.75. Selection
decides where the constant-size step points, not how big it is.
